classification_rationale: A contract upgrade marked the zero hash as a trusted Merkle root, introducing a validation-gap in the bridge's message-proof verification: any message with a zero-bytes proof passes as valid. The first attacker drained 100 WBTC (~$2.3M) by submitting a valid-looking but forged cross-chain withdrawal message. Over 40 copycats replayed modified versions of the same transaction, draining $190M total. The entire class of attacks relies on the validation-gap in the proof-verification lifecycle, not on basic DeFi action composition.
